The '''U2-C1-series housekeeping droid''' was manufactured by [[Publictechnic]] and sold for commercial use as a [[Janitor/Legends|janitor]] and maid. They were common sights throughout [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]] and often fell beneath the notice of most individuals. This trait made the U2-C1 useful to criminal elements, who reprogrammed the droids to commit crimes.

==Description==
U2-C1-series housekeeping droids had a vaguely [[Humanoid/Legends|humanoid]] look, with two long, spindly [[Limb/Legends|legs]] holding up a frame cast in hardened [[Color/Legends|blue]] [[Plastic/Legends|plastic]].<ref name="EGTD" /> Standing under a [[Meter/Legends|meter]] tall,<ref name="AEG" /> the U2-C1 had a hunched look with a drooping head.<ref name="EGTD" />

The head was equipped with a [[Photoreceptor/Legends|optical slot]], [[Audio sensor/Legends|audio sensors]], and a [[Vocabulator/Legends|vocabulator]] that allowed the droid to communicate in [[Galactic Basic Standard/Legends|Basic]]. The U2-C1's [[Behavioral circuitry matrix/Legends|behavioral circuitry]] was located within the droid's head and neck. The bulk of the droid's torso was taken up by a waste-storage bin which could be emptied via a discharge hatch on the ventral surface.<ref name="EGTD" />

The main feature of the U2-C1 was its arms. Each was a powerful [[Vacuum cleaner|vacuum sweeper]] constructed of black [[flex-tubing]] that was attached to the torso and allowed the droid to vacuum up debris into its internal waste-storage bin. A U2-C1's right arm was also fitted with an attachment to clean upholstery, and the left arm was equipped with a stiff wire brush intended to remove persistent stains.<ref name="EGTD" />

To keep costs down, the U2-C1 was not designed to look aesthetically pleasing, and it was given simple utilitarian functionality as opposed to pleasant [[Personality matrix/Legends|personality programming]].<ref name="EGTD" /> Each unit retailed for seven hundred and fifty [[Galactic Credit Standard/Legends|credits]].<ref name="AEG" />

==Role==
[[Publictechnic]] designed the U2-C1 to cater to the commercial droid market, and it was not marketed directly to consumers. Its cheap and efficient nature meant that it was popular with [[Hotel/Legends|hotel]] managers and [[Janitor/Legends|janitorial]] services. Requiring little supervision, U2-C1s were often employed as janitors and maids, performing the same cleaning duties night after night.<ref name="EGTD" />

==History==
U2-C1s were often sold to medium-sized businesses in discounted "fleet sales." By the time of the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]], Publictechnic had very little issues with the U2-C1-series. User complaints of a serious nature were rare, and while the droid's external parts broke off easily, replacement parts were cheap and easy to obtain.<ref name="EGTD" />

Despite the droid's slightly sinister appearance, the U2-C1 was a common sight throughout [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]] and did not attract undue attention. This trait led to the droid being exploited by criminals including [[Thief/Legends|thieves]] and [[Gangster/Legends|gangsters]]. It was discovered that the housekeeping software in the U2-C1-series was easy to exploit<ref name="EGTD" /> often by redefining what the droid considered to be trash.<ref name="AEG" />

A U2-C1 droid reprogrammed by criminals was ordered to enter hotel rooms and directed to use its vacuum sweepers to steal jewelry and [[credit voucher]]s from guests. Another U2-C1 vacuumed a [[timer bomb]] into its internal bin which subsequently exploded outside the office of a [[Corporate Sector/Legends|Corporate Sector]] executive. [[U2-C1]], a U2-C1-series droid working in [[Jabba's Palace/Legends|Jabba's Palace]] on [[Tatooine/Legends|Tatooine]], was a [[Spy/Legends|spy]] and [[Courier/Legends|courier]] working for [[Jabba Desilijic Tiure/Legends|Jabba's]] rival, [[Lady/Legends|Lady]] [[Valarian/Legends|Valarian]]. The droid would pass covert messages to other [[Agent/Legends|agents]] working for Valarian in the palace, including the [[Whiphid/Legends|Whiphid]] hunter [[J'Quille/Legends|J'Quille]]. To prevent anyone else from listening in to the messages, U2-C1 used the noise from its vacuum sweepers as cover.<ref name="EGTD" />
